title: Browser Automation
summary: Provides full browser automation through multiple backends (Browserbase, Browser Use, Firecrawl, Camofox, local Chrome via CDP) with accessibility-tree-based page interaction and tools for navigation, clicking, typing, vision analysis, and raw CDP access.

env_vars.name: BROWSER_USE_API_KEY
env_vars.purpose: Browser Use cloud browser API key

env_vars.name: CAMOFOX_USER_ID
env_vars.purpose: Camofox userId for externally managed sessions

env_vars.name: CAMOFOX_ADOPT_EXISTING_TAB
env_vars.purpose: Adopt existing Camofox tab instead of creating new

env_vars.name: BROWSERBASE_PROXIES
env_vars.purpose: Enable residential proxies for Browserbase (default true)

env_vars.name: BROWSER_INACTIVITY_TIMEOUT
env_vars.purpose: Inactivity timeout before auto-cleanup in seconds

cli_commands.purpose: Connect browser tools to local Chrome via CDP
Connect to a specific CDP endpoint
Detach from CDP and return to cloud/local mode

config_keys.description: Cloud browser provider: browserbase, browser_use, or firecrawl
Auto-route private/LAN URLs to local sidecar
Allow cloud provider to attempt private URLs
Record browser sessions as WebM video
JS dialog handling: must_respond, auto_dismiss, or auto_accept
Enable persistent Camofox browser sessions (must be nested under browser.camofox)

slash_commands.purpose: Connect to Chrome via CDP (CLI only)

code_examples.caption: Run Camofox with Docker
code_examples.code:
git clone https://github.com/jo-inc/camofox-browser
cd camofox-browser
make up

gotchas: `browser.camofox.managed_persistence` must be nested correctly — a top-level `managed_persistence: true` is silently ignored
`/browser connect` is a CLI slash command only — does not work in gateway/WebUI/Telegram/Discord
WSL2 + Windows Chrome: prefer chrome-devtools-mcp over /browser connect
Without `--user-data-dir`, starting Chrome while another instance is running won't open port 9222
Snapshots over 8000 characters are automatically LLM-summarized — may lose detail
Cloud providers (Browserbase, Browser Use, Firecrawl) do not expose CDP to browser_cdp tool
If paid Browserbase features aren't available, Hermes auto-falls back (keepAlive → proxies)
Camoufox is the stealth backend shipped in v0.7.0 (github.com/daijro/camoufox) — anti-fingerprint, auto-installs via `hermes tools`. See community-camoufox
full_text:
# Browser Automation

Hermes Agent includes a full browser automation toolset with multiple backend options:

- **Browserbase cloud mode** — managed cloud browsers with anti-bot tooling
- **Browser Use cloud mode** — alternative cloud browser provider
- **Firecrawl cloud mode** — cloud browsers with built-in scraping
- **Camofox local mode** — local anti-detection browsing (Firefox-based fingerprint spoofing)
- **Local Chrome via CDP** — connect to your own Chrome instance using `/browser connect`
- **Local browser mode** — `agent-browser` CLI with local Chromium

Pages are represented as **accessibility trees** (text-based snapshots). Interactive elements get ref IDs (like `@e1`, `@e2`) for clicking and typing.

## Setup

Nous Portal subscribers can use browser automation through the Tool Gateway without separate API keys.

```bash
# ~/.hermes/.env
BROWSERBASE_API_KEY=***
BROWSERBASE_PROJECT_ID=your-project-id
BROWSER_USE_API_KEY=***       # Alternative provider
FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=fc-***      # Firecrawl provider
CAMOFOX_URL=http://localhost:9377  # Local Camofox
```

## Hybrid Routing

When a cloud provider is configured, Hermes auto-spawns a local Chromium sidecar for private/LAN URLs (localhost, 192.168.x.x, etc.). Public URLs use the cloud provider.

```yaml
browser:
  cloud_provider: browserbase
  auto_local_for_private_urls: false  # disable hybrid routing
```

## Local Chrome via CDP (`/browser connect`)

```
/browser connect              # Connect to Chrome at ws://localhost:9222
/browser connect ws://host:port  # Connect to specific endpoint
/browser status               # Check current connection
/browser disconnect           # Detach
```

Note: `/browser connect` is CLI-only — not available in gateway/WebUI contexts.

## Available Tools

- **`browser_navigate`** — Navigate to a URL (must be called first)
- **`browser_snapshot`** — Get accessibility tree snapshot; `full=true` for complete content
- **`browser_click`** — Click element by ref ID (e.g., `@e5`)
- **`browser_type`** — Type text into input field (clears first)
- **`browser_scroll`** — Scroll up or down
- **`browser_press`** — Press keyboard key (Enter, Tab, Escape, etc.)
- **`browser_back`** — Navigate back
- **`browser_get_images`** — List all images on page
- **`browser_vision`** — Screenshot + AI vision analysis
- **`browser_console`** — Get JS console output; evaluate JS expressions
- **`browser_cdp`** — Raw CDP passthrough (CDP endpoint required)
- **`browser_dialog`** — Respond to native JS dialogs

## Session Management

- Each task gets an isolated browser session
- Auto-cleanup after 2 minutes of inactivity (background check every 30s)
- Emergency cleanup on process exit
- Session recording: `browser.record_sessions: true`

## Limitations

- Text-based only (accessibility tree, not pixel coordinates)
- Snapshots over 8000 characters are LLM-summarized
- No file downloads
- Cloud sessions consume provider credits
